title: Collection size and array length comparisons should make sense
description: Collection size and array length comparisons should make senseAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearreliabilityBugQuick FixIDE quick fixes available with SonarQube for IDEtype-dependentWhy is this an issue?The size of a collection and the length of an array are always greater than or equal to zero. Testing it doesn’t make sense, since the result is alwaystrue.if (someSet.size >= 0) {...} // Noncompliant always true const result = someArray.length >= 0; // Noncompliant always trueSimilarly testing that it is less than zero will always returnfalse.if (someMap.size < 0) {...} // Noncompliant always falseFix the code to properly check for emptiness if it was the intent, or remove the redundant code to keep the current behavior.Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in you
recommendation: Remove the problematic code and refactor as needed.

compliant:
if (someSet.size >= 0) {...} // Noncompliant always true

const result = someArray.length >= 0;  // Noncompliant always true

checks.message: Collection size/length comparison that is always true or always false. Use proper emptiness checks instead.
